chapters.name: Statistics
chapters.description: Mean and interpretation of grouped data.
chapters.lessonSummary: Read grouped tables carefully and compute representative values.
chapters.practiceFocus: Class mark, mean, and frequency reasoning
chapters.checkpointGoal: Calculate and interpret grouped-data measures correctly.

questions.prompt: Find the mean of the data: 5, 7, 9, 11.
questions.explanation: Add the observations and divide by the number of observations.

questions.prompt: Find the mean of the data: 12, 14, 16, 18.
questions.explanation: Add the observations and divide by the number of observations.

questions.prompt: Find the mean of the data: 3, 6, 9, 12, 15.
questions.explanation: Add the observations and divide by the number of observations.

questions.prompt: Find the mean of the data: 10, 20, 30.
questions.explanation: Add the observations and divide by the number of observations.

questions.prompt: Find the mean of the data: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20.
questions.explanation: Add the observations and divide by the number of observations.

questions.prompt: Find the mean of the data: 6, 8, 10, 12.
questions.explanation: Add the observations and divide by the number of observations.

questions.prompt: Find the median of the ordered data: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10.
questions.explanation: Use the middle term or average of the two middle terms.

questions.prompt: Find the median of the ordered data: 5, 7, 9, 11, 13.
questions.explanation: Use the middle term or average of the two middle terms.

questions.prompt: Find the median of the ordered data: 1, 3, 5, 7.
questions.explanation: Use the middle term or average of the two middle terms.

questions.prompt: Find the median of the ordered data: 10, 12, 14, 16.
questions.explanation: Use the middle term or average of the two middle terms.

questions.prompt: Find the median of the ordered data: 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13.
questions.explanation: Use the middle term or average of the two middle terms.

questions.prompt: Find the median of the ordered data: 8, 10, 12, 14, 16.
questions.explanation: Use the middle term or average of the two middle terms.

questions.prompt: Which observation occurs most frequently in a data set?
questions.explanation: Mode is the most frequent observation.

questions.prompt: The class mark of an interval is the:
questions.explanation: Class mark = (upper + lower)/2.

questions.prompt: Which graph is often used to represent cumulative frequency?
questions.explanation: An ogive represents cumulative frequency.

questions.prompt: A histogram is mainly used for:
questions.explanation: Histograms show grouped continuous data.

questions.prompt: The range of a data set is:
questions.explanation: Range measures spread.

questions.prompt: For ordered data with an odd number of observations, the median is:
questions.explanation: The median is the central observation.

questions.prompt: Mean = sum of observations divided by the number of ____.
questions.explanation: This is the definition of arithmetic mean.

questions.prompt: Median is the ____ value when data are arranged in order.
questions.explanation: Median is positional average.

questions.prompt: A data set can have more than one mode.
questions.explanation: Some data sets are bimodal or multimodal.

questions.prompt: Range gives an idea of dispersion in a data set.
questions.explanation: It measures spread between extremes.

questions.prompt: Class mark is also called the class ____.
questions.explanation: It is the average of class limits.

questions.prompt: Bars in a histogram usually touch each other for continuous data.
questions.explanation: Continuous intervals are adjacent.

questions.prompt: The mean of five numbers is 12. What is their total sum?
questions.explanation: Total = mean x number of observations.

questions.prompt: How many observations are there if the median is the average of the 5th and 6th terms?
questions.explanation: That happens when the count is even and equal to 10 here.

questions.prompt: The marks 6, 7, 7, 8, 9, 9, 9, 10 are listed. What is the mode?
questions.explanation: 9 occurs most frequently.

questions.prompt: For the interval 20-30, what is the class mark?
questions.explanation: Class mark = (20 + 30)/2.

questions.prompt: Find the range of 14, 18, 11, 25, 20.
questions.explanation: Range = 25 - 11.

questions.prompt: A new observation 20 is added to the data 10, 10, 10, 10. What is the new mean?
questions.explanation: New sum is 60 and number of observations is 5.
